entries.title: 北宋汴京小本起家账房法
entries.summary: 在北宋汴京这类高流通都市，最值钱的不是奇技淫巧，而是更稳的账、更快的周转和更能复购的小生意。
steps: 先选纸笔、针线、皂角、茶点这类高频货，不碰重资产大作坊。
先用固定标价和干净账本赢信用，再做赊销与熟客名单。
先靠客栈、码头、书坊和脚店找稳定客流，再谈扩张。
contraindications: 不要一开局就吹大炼钢铁、蒸汽机这类跨时代神话。
不要把银钱全压在一票货上，更不要碰来路不明的官货。
不要让人觉得你像妖人或投机骗子。
entries.escalation: 当现金流稳定后，升级到批零结合、代记账和渠道分销。
firstMoves: 先租最小摊位，做高频小货组合包。
先把账记得比同行清楚，把回头客认出来。
先结识一个愿意替你背书的掌柜或书吏。
entries.payoff: 三个月目标不是称霸全城，而是把你从街边小贩升级成有账、有熟客、有供货关系的准掌柜。
fatalMistakes: 别急着炫耀超时代知识。
别在没保护伞前碰盐、铁、军需这类高风险领域。
entries.coverStory: 自称跟过外地掌柜学过清账和定价，只是做事更细。

entries.title: Northern Song Market Ladder
entries.summary: In a city like Northern Song Kaifeng, the real edge is not a miracle invention. It is cleaner books, faster turnover, and trust in repeat daily goods.
steps: Open with paper, needles, soap stock, tea snacks, or other fast-repeat goods instead of a heavy workshop.
Use fixed prices and visibly clean ledgers before you extend credit.
Anchor yourself near inns, book markets, docks, or busy alley commerce first.
contraindications: Do not open with steam engines, firearms, or other impossible leaps.
Do not tie all capital into one shipment or touch controlled goods too early.
Do not sound like a prophet or an eccentric outsider.
entries.escalation: Once cash flow is stable, move into wholesale links, record keeping for others, and small credit.
firstMoves: Rent the smallest viable stall and sell repeat-use bundles.
Keep books cleaner than rivals and identify repeat customers by name.
Win one credible backer such as a shopkeeper, broker, or clerk.
entries.payoff: The first win is not becoming the richest man alive. It is becoming the most orderly trader on your block, then buying better position from that base.
fatalMistakes: Do not reveal future knowledge as magic.
Do not touch salt, iron, military, or tax-sensitive goods before you have cover.
entries.coverStory: Say you were trained by a traveling merchant who obsessed over clean books and reliable stock.

entries.title: Regency London First Fortune Line
entries.summary: In Regency London, the first fortune usually comes from clean books, discreet commissions, and repeat urban services long before it comes from grand invention.
steps: Start with copying, bookkeeping, broker errands, discreet shopping, or introduction work instead of trying to play gentleman investor.
Put yourself near inns, coffeehouses, law offices, docks, and busy shopping streets where literate reliability is rare and useful.
Win repeat patrons through punctual delivery, neat invoices, and silence about other people's business.
contraindications: Do not tie all your money into one speculative cargo or fashionable craze.
Do not pretend to be grander than your accent, clothes, and references can support.
Do not talk politics or finance like an insider before you have cover.
entries.escalation: Once trust and repeat custom are stable, move from errands into agency work, supply contracts, and small credit.
entries.eraLabel: Regency Britain / 摄政时代英国
firstMoves: Rent the cheapest respectable room you can manage near foot traffic.
Sell one reliable service that clerks, widows, officers, or shopkeepers need every week.
Track who pays on time, who introduces, and who leaks gossip.
entries.payoff: Your first durable win is becoming the person respectable people trust with small but constant business.
fatalMistakes: Do not arrive sounding like a visionary banker.
Do not chase aristocratic glamour before you have cash flow.
entries.coverStory: Say you learned orderly books and reliable errands in a provincial counting house and came to London to make yourself useful.

entries.title: 北宋汴京从账房到上位的阶梯
entries.summary: 在汴京这种权力密集区，真正的上位不是先争名位，而是先变成某个强者离不开的账房、文书或耳目。
steps: 先去粮仓、税务、库房、书院、行会找最缺会算账和会写文书的位置。
先替人省钱、省麻烦、补漏洞，再慢慢掌握名单和消息。
先把自己做成可靠工具，再借人情往上换身份。
contraindications: 不要过早站队到最显眼的派系。
不要把夺权野心挂在脸上。
不要在还没进核心圈前评价朝局。
entries.escalation: 掌握账目、人手与信息后，再争取推荐、官身或军需口。
firstMoves: 先找一个缺文书和算账的人跟。
先做能立刻省钱省时的小改进。
先记住谁负责钱、粮、人和消息。
entries.payoff: 你真正的第一阶不是做官，而是先碰到账和名单；一旦你掌握这两样，地位会自己长出来。
fatalMistakes: 别急着谈治国大道理。
别在没有保荐时跨级表现。
entries.coverStory: 说自己出身寒门但跟过严厉先生，最擅长清账、抄录和补漏。

entries.title: Northern Song Patronage Ladder
entries.summary: In a capital like Kaifeng, the real rise-to-power line starts by becoming indispensable in ledgers, paperwork, logistics, and names before you ever reach office.
steps: Move toward warehouses, tax offices, supply rooms, academies, or guild work where literate hands are scarce.
Save a patron money, embarrassment, or delay before you ask for rank.
Track who controls funds, grain, staffing, and introductions.
contraindications: Do not rush into the most visible faction first.
Do not advertise ambition to rule.
Do not comment on high politics before you have cover.
entries.escalation: Once you control records, people, or flow of goods, trade usefulness for recommendation and office.
firstMoves: Attach yourself to whoever urgently needs books, copies, or logistics sorted.
Deliver one practical fix that saves time or leakage.
Map the chain of money, grain, and introductions.
entries.payoff: Power first flows through records and access. Office usually arrives after usefulness becomes painful to replace.
fatalMistakes: Do not perform genius in public before you are protected.
Do not jump above your sponsor too early.
entries.coverStory: Present yourself as a disciplined low-born clerk trained to clean accounts and calm confusion.

entries.title: Tudor London Patronage Ladder
entries.summary: In Tudor London, the safest rise begins inside a household, office, or counting room where letters, favors, and introductions matter more than birth.
steps: Enter through a noble or merchant household as a clerk, scrivener, steward's assistant, or discreet runner.
Make yourself useful in letters, provisioning, lists, and quiet problem-solving before you ever ask for promotion.
Learn who seals papers, who moves money, who controls access, and who carries gossip.
contraindications: Do not attach yourself to the loudest faction first.
Do not speak about religion, succession, or statecraft before you know the room.
Do not advertise that you mean to rise.
entries.escalation: When stronger people trust you with papers, provisions, and messages, influence arrives before title.
firstMoves: Attach yourself to the person drowning in letters, bills, or provisioning mistakes.
Fix one leak in time, money, or embarrassment before you ask for trust.
Map the chain from chamber to kitchen to chapel to counting room.
entries.payoff: The first real ascent is becoming difficult to replace in the rooms where orders become action.
fatalMistakes: Do not sound like you already deserve office.
Do not jump above your patron before you are shielded.
entries.coverStory: Present yourself as a sober household hand who writes clearly, remembers details, and keeps panic out of the room.

entries.title: 晚清通商口岸第一桶金路线
entries.summary: 晚清上海这类通商口岸，最稳的首富线不是办大厂，而是先吃信息差、结算差和包装标准化的红利。
steps: 先做翻译、跑单、代记账、代采购这类轻资产服务。
先盯进口货、国货、行会价和码头价之间的价差。
先把包装、标签、账单和交货时间做得比别人稳定。
contraindications: 不要一开始就重资产押厂房和机器。
不要碰鸦片、军火、走私这类最容易暴死的赛道。
不要同时得罪洋行、地痞和衙门。
entries.escalation: 当你掌握票据、客户和信誉后，再反向控制货源与分销。
firstMoves: 先接最短链条的代办和撮合活。
先用可靠交货和清楚账单换口碑。
先锁定一个货类，再扩第二条线。
entries.payoff: 你的第一桶金来自周转速度和撮合费，不是来自空想中的工业帝国。
fatalMistakes: 别急着大规模借债。
别把未来市场趋势说得像天机。
entries.coverStory: 对外说自己只是更懂记账、交货和货样整理，不是通天奇才。

entries.title: Treaty Port First Fortune Line
entries.summary: In Late Qing Shanghai or any treaty port, the fastest realistic fortune line begins with brokerage, settlement discipline, and packaging consistency before heavy industry.
steps: Start with translation, paperwork, sourcing, broker runs, or account handling instead of buying machines.
Exploit the spread between dock price, guild price, imported stock, and inland resale.
Make labels, invoices, delivery promises, and inventory discipline more reliable than your rivals.
contraindications: Do not begin by sinking capital into a factory.
Do not touch opium, arms, or smuggling lanes unless you want short-term death or ruin.
Do not antagonize foreign firms, local muscle, and magistrates at the same time.
entries.escalation: After you own the customer relationship and paperwork flow, move upstream into supply and downstream into distribution.
firstMoves: Take the shortest commission work that teaches prices and trust chains.
Win one repeat client through clean delivery and cleaner books.
Own one product lane before you widen into a second.
entries.payoff: The first fortune comes from faster turnover and brokerage fees, not from instantly founding an empire.
fatalMistakes: Do not lever up too early.
Do not speak about the future like prophecy.
entries.coverStory: Present yourself as a disciplined trade assistant who is unusually strict about books, labels, and timing.

entries.title: 晚清口岸从商人到上位者的转身
entries.summary: 晚清口岸的上位线，不是先做大人物，而是先成为洋行、商帮、衙门和士绅之间的必经节点。
steps: 先掌握票据、货单、翻译、货运和税务流程。
先替一方解决别人解决不了的协同问题，再索取位置。
先结识会保你的人，再结识会用你的人。
contraindications: 不要过早公开表态站队。
不要在没人担保时碰军政机密。
不要把自己包装成救世主。
entries.escalation: 当你能调度资金、消息和人脉时，影响力会先于正式名分到来。
firstMoves: 先把一条复杂流程跑顺。
先让关键人物省心，而不是先求头衔。
先记住谁能签字、谁能放货、谁能压事。
entries.payoff: 一旦你成了跨势力的翻译器和结算器，你就能从外围人变成牌桌上的常驻位置。
fatalMistakes: 别同时吃两家看不见的底牌。
别在羽翼未丰前公开议政。
entries.coverStory: 对外只说自己擅长账务、交涉和让复杂流程少出错。

entries.title: Treaty Port Influence Ladder
entries.summary: The Late Qing power line in a treaty port begins when you become the necessary connector between firms, guilds, magistrates, and local prestige networks.
steps: Master invoices, manifests, freight timing, translation, and tax handling first.
Solve one coordination problem that no faction can solve alone.
Find protectors before you seek titles.
contraindications: Do not declare your faction too early.
Do not touch military or political secrets before you have backing.
Do not style yourself as a savior.
entries.escalation: Once money, information, and introductions run through you, influence arrives before formal rank.
firstMoves: Untangle one messy process from contract to payment.
Make a strong patron more efficient before you ask for elevation.
Map who can sign, release, delay, or protect.
entries.payoff: When several factions need you to understand one another, you stop being expendable and start becoming political furniture.
fatalMistakes: Do not play double games you cannot hide.
Do not speak like a statesman before you are shielded.
entries.coverStory: Describe yourself as a practical fixer for books, negotiation, and timing, not a visionary operator.

entries.title: 能落地的现代知识外挂
entries.summary: 真正好用的现代外挂，往往不是大发明，而是账本模板、标准化工序、包装、标签、抽检和交付纪律。
steps: 先挑低门槛工艺，例如肥皂、印刷、包装、发酵、仓储、度量统一。
先把步骤写成谁都能照做的流程，不靠你亲自盯。
先卖更稳定、更省料、更少返工，而不是卖未来神话。
contraindications: 不要上来就谈蒸汽机、机床体系和跨时代重工业。
不要在材料链没打通前承诺产量。
不要让人知道你的方法远超时代常识。
entries.escalation: 先用轻工业和管理外挂挣钱，再慢慢推进更重的制造路线。
entries.eraLabel: 前工业到近代 / Preindustrial to Early Industrial
firstMoves: 先做一本标准账册和交货表。
先挑一个能马上降低返工率的流程。
先把结果卖给最在意稳定性的客户。
entries.payoff: 最稳的外挂收益，是别人开始依赖你的标准和流程，而不是依赖你的单次灵感。
fatalMistakes: 别把技术说成神授。
别在工艺没跑稳前扩大规模。
entries.coverStory: 说自己只是把师傅们零散经验整理成更不容易出错的做法。

entries.title: Modern Edge That Can Actually Land
entries.summary: The best modern edge is usually not a grand invention. It is the stack of ledgers, standards, packaging, inspection, process notes, and delivery discipline.
steps: Choose low-barrier gains first: soap, printing, packaging, fermentation, warehousing, accounting, or standardized measures.
Write the method so ordinary workers can repeat it without your constant supervision.
Sell fewer defects, steadier quality, and cleaner delivery instead of miracle language.
contraindications: Do not open with steam engines, precision machine tools, or impossible heavy industry.
Do not promise output before the material chain exists.
Do not let people conclude that your knowledge is unnatural.
entries.escalation: Use light-industry and management wins to finance heavier manufacturing later.
entries.eraLabel: Preindustrial to Early Industrial / 前工业到近代
firstMoves: Create one standard ledger and one delivery checklist.
Pick one process improvement that visibly reduces waste.
Sell the result to buyers who value consistency most.
entries.payoff: The durable gain comes when others depend on your system, not just your individual brilliance.
fatalMistakes: Do not describe technique as divine revelation.
Do not scale before the process is stable.
entries.coverStory: Say you are simply good at organizing scattered workshop wisdom into repeatable routines.

entries.title: Victorian London Modern Edge
entries.summary: In Victorian London, the profitable modern edge is process discipline: packaging, hygiene, batch control, scheduling, and cleaner books that workshops can adopt right now.
steps: Start with packaging, soap, inventory control, print workflows, delivery schedules, or inspection routines instead of impossible machinery leaps.
Write the process so an ordinary foreman or apprentice can repeat it without your constant supervision.
Sell steadier quality, cleaner handling, and lower waste to buyers who already hate inconsistency.
contraindications: Do not promise a miracle machine you cannot actually build.
Do not scale before raw materials, labor, and delivery timing are stable.
Do not describe your method as futuristic revelation.
entries.escalation: Use those light-industry wins to finance better tooling, better premises, and eventually heavier manufacturing.
entries.eraLabel: Victorian Britain / 维多利亚时代英国
firstMoves: Build one standard ledger and one delivery checklist first.
Choose one process change that visibly cuts waste or spoilage in the first week.
Sell consistency to the buyers already annoyed by sloppy competitors.
entries.payoff: The durable gain is becoming the workshop with fewer surprises, not the workshop with the loudest promise.
fatalMistakes: Do not skip proof, sampling, or inspection.
Do not let anyone think you can leap straight past the factory system.
entries.coverStory: Say you are obsessive about tidy books, clean handling, and repeatable workshop routines, nothing more mystical than that.

entries.title: 刚穿过去先别显圣
entries.summary: 多数穿越者最先死的不是没本事，而是说错话、行错礼、银钱单位弄错，像个来历不明的怪人。
steps: 先学称呼、口音、钱币、地名和常见礼节。
先观察别人怎么穿、怎么行礼、怎么讨价还价，再模仿。
先给自己编一条稳定来路，只讲七分，不讲十成。
contraindications: 不要主动提未来、科技树和历史走向。
不要一上来就显得比当地读书人还懂天下。
不要随便碰禁忌职业、宗教和礼法。
entries.escalation: 先像当地人，再像有用的人，最后才像不能轻易得罪的人。
entries.eraLabel: 通用历史场景 / General Historical
firstMoves: 先学钱和称呼。
先抄习惯，不要急着秀见识。
先编一个别人愿意信的来历。
entries.payoff: 只要前三天不穿帮，你就有机会把异常变成优势，而不是把优势变成死因。
fatalMistakes: 别自称天命之人。
别让人觉得你像细作、邪人或疯子。
entries.coverStory: 优先选择外地学徒、落魄亲戚、逃荒后投亲这类普通而可查得不深的身份。

entries.title: Do Not Arrive Like a Miracle
entries.summary: Most time travelers die first because they speak wrong, bow wrong, price wrong, and look like an unexplained anomaly.
steps: Learn forms of address, money units, place names, and visible etiquette first.
Watch how people dress, bargain, and greet before you try to impress them.
Build a stable origin story and only tell seven parts of ten.
contraindications: Do not volunteer future history or a technology tree.
Do not sound more informed than the local educated class on day one.
Do not trip religious, legal, or ritual taboos casually.
entries.escalation: Look local first, useful second, and formidable only much later.
entries.eraLabel: General Historical / 通用历史场景
firstMoves: Learn money and titles first.
Imitate before you display knowledge.
Pick one ordinary backstory and repeat it consistently.
entries.payoff: If you survive the first three days without looking uncanny, your anomaly can become leverage instead of a death sentence.
fatalMistakes: Do not claim divine destiny.
Do not look like a spy, cultist, or madman.
entries.coverStory: Use an ordinary identity such as a distant apprentice, displaced relative, or poor traveler with a thin but believable background.

entries.title: Medieval London Blend-In Protocol
entries.summary: In medieval London, survival starts with sounding ordinary: coin handling, prayers, rank awareness, and visible obedience matter before any clever plan does.
steps: Learn how people address superiors, handle money, swear oaths, and queue before you try to stand out.
Copy clothing layers, walking pace, meal habits, and bargaining rhythm before you show skill.
Pick one thin but ordinary backstory and repeat it until it feels boring.
contraindications: Do not sound cleaner, freer, or more opinionated than the street around you on day one.
Do not improvise on religion, guild custom, or legal ritual.
Do not let anyone think you appeared from nowhere.
entries.escalation: Once you look ordinary, you can become useful. Once you become useful, you can become hard to discard.
entries.eraLabel: Medieval England / 中世纪英格兰
firstMoves: Learn money, prayers, and forms of address before you trade stories.
Find the safest ordinary place to eat, sleep, and listen.
Let other people underestimate you until your cover story hardens.
entries.payoff: If you pass as merely poor and local enough, you buy time to turn strangeness into advantage later.
fatalMistakes: Do not speak like you expect equality or privacy.
Do not look like a heretic, spy, or wandering madman.
entries.coverStory: Use a forgettable identity such as a displaced apprentice, an unlucky relative, or a traveler looking for service.

entries.title: 钱币文字器物线索速查
entries.summary: 看到钱币、文字、衣料、印章和器物时，不要急着下结论，先抓最能暴露时代、地域和身份的硬线索。
steps: 先看材质、边缘磨损、铸造或印刷方式。
再看字形、称谓、年号、徽记和纹样。
最后看谁会携带这东西，它更像市井货、官用物还是宗教物。
contraindications: 不要只凭一个字就断言朝代。
不要把现代修复痕迹当原始时代特征。
entries.escalation: 把看到的线索继续拆成材质、文字、用途和佩戴者四类，再回来问 Timet。
firstMoves: 先截取最清楚的局部。
先数重复出现的符号和称谓。
先判断它是流通物还是身份物。
entries.payoff: 你不一定立刻知道准确年份，但可以先排除大半错误时代和错误身份。
fatalMistakes: 别只凭模糊轮廓就下注。
别忽略持有者身份线索。
entries.coverStory: 对外只说你在帮人辨认旧物，不要说你靠它判断天下格局。

entries.title: Coin, Script, and Artifact Clue Pass
entries.summary: When you see a coin, script sample, seal, textile, or tool, do not jump to the dynasty first. Start with the hardest clues for era, region, and social rank.
steps: Check material, edge wear, casting or printing method first.
Then check script form, titles, reign markers, badges, and repeating motifs.
Finally ask who would carry it: common trade, official issue, military, temple, or household use.
contraindications: Do not date an object from one character alone.
Do not mistake modern repair marks for original period traits.
entries.escalation: Break the clue into material, script, function, and owner class, then ask Timet again with those four buckets.
firstMoves: Crop the sharpest detail first.
Count repeated symbols, titles, and motifs.
Decide whether the object signals trade, office, religion, or rank.
entries.payoff: You may not know the exact year yet, but you can quickly eliminate the wrong era and the wrong social layer.
fatalMistakes: Do not bet everything on a blurry silhouette.
Do not ignore who would normally own the object.
entries.coverStory: Say you are trying to identify an old item, not reading the whole political order from it.
